title: Documentation, APIs, Licenses, and Generated-Artifact Gates · Repository Template Mission
summary: A systems tools team. The team needs a native CLI repo with warnings, tests, fuzz-ready boundaries, sanitizers, documentation, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Deliver Sanitized Native CLI Template with acceptance evidence, rubric defense, reflection, and a changed-constraint extension. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use AddressSanitizer builds to detect memory safety defects in native code.
Use UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer with appropriate recover or trap policy.
Run expensive dynamic checks in a documented matrix with symbolized actionable output.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# Risk-profiled repository gate portfolio
# Each gate needs purpose, command, failure fixture, owner, and suppression policy.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a gates-artifact-provenance-1ml211: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Artifact Provenance into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
gates-artifact-provenance-1ml211:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]

options.text: Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.

steps.title: Defend the stakeholder delivery
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Artifact Provenance applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record build provenance, checksums, signatures, and dependency inventories when risk requires.
